projectDescription: A high-performance, cloud-native, AI-powered time-series database built for Industrial IoT, capable of ingesting and processing petabytes of data per day from billions of sensors.
keyFeatures: One table per device data model with supertables for cross-device aggregation
High-performance ingestion and querying — up to 10.6x faster than competing TSDBs
Built-in stream processing engine for real-time event-driven analytics
Native data subscription with Kafka-compatible consumer API
TDgpt AI agent for time-series forecasting, anomaly detection, and imputation
Zero-code data connectors for MQTT, OPC, Kafka, InfluxDB, and more
Schemaless ingestion via InfluxDB Line Protocol, OpenTSDB Telnet/JSON
Cloud-native distributed architecture with Kubernetes support and RAFT consensus
Tiered storage with S3 support and 10:1 data compression
REST API, JDBC/ODBC, and multi-language client libraries (C/C++, Java, Python, Go, Rust, Node.js)
publicApiSurface: taos_connect() / taos_close() — C client library connection API
taos_schemaless_insert() — schemaless InfluxDB/OpenTSDB line protocol ingestion
taos_subscribe() / taos_consume() — data subscription consumer API
CREATE TABLE / CREATE STABLE — DDL for tables and supertables
INSERT INTO ... USING ... TAGS — auto-create child table on insert
SELECT ... FROM ... INTERVAL() FILL() SLIMIT() — time-series SQL extensions
CREATE STREAM ... AS SELECT ... — stream processing DDL
CREATE TOPIC / taos_tmq_new_consumer() — TMQ (TDengine Message Queue) API
REST API /rest/sql — HTTP endpoint for SQL execution
taosAdapter HTTP API — RESTful data ingestion and query interface
User-Defined Functions (UDF) in C and Python
